ocr.text: In the context of labor productivity metrics within the Asia-Pacific construction sector, it is essential to evaluate various
performance indicators that reflect operational efficiency and sustainability. The upcoming data table delineates crucial metrics
for Skanska AB, illustrating projected trends from 2023 to 2025.
LaborProductivityMetrics  PerformanceRatios  Year-over-YearChange
MetricName  2023  2024  2025
LaborCostEfficiency  [HIDDEN]  77.5%  [HIDDEN]
WorkforceUtilization  82.3%  84.1%  85.0%
ProjectCompletionRate  90.0%  92.5%  93.0%
AverageHoursWorked  1,800  1,850  1,900
EmployeeTurnover  [HIDDEN]  11.5%  11.0%
TrainingInvestment  [HIDDEN]  ¥1,600M  ¥1,700M
[HIDDEN]  5.2%  4.8%  [HIDDEN]
EquipmentUtilization  85.0%  87.0%  88.5%
ClientSatisfactionScore  78.0%  80.0%  [HIDDEN]
Notably, the "TrainingInvestment" for 2023 was ¥1,500M, underscoring the company's commitment to enhancing workforce
capabilities as a means to improve overall productivity. Furthermore, the "LaborCostEfficiency" metric shows an increase from
75.2% in 2023 to a projected 78.8% in 2025, indicating a positive trajectory towards optimizing labor expenses relative to
output. Alongside financial metrics, the company is also focused on sustainability, with the "MaterialWasteRatio" expected to
decrease from its current measures to 4.5% by 2025, showcasing a commitment to reducing waste in construction processes.
Additionally, employee engagement is highlighted through an "EmployeeTurnover" rate of 12.0% in 2023, which the
organization aims to reduce further by fostering a more supportive work environment. Lastly, the projected
"ClientSatisfactionScore" for 2025 is set at 81.5%, reflecting Skanska AB's focus on delivering high-quality services that meet
client expectations while maintaining a safe work environment, as indicated by the "SafetyIncidentRate" metrics. Collectively,
these metrics provide a comprehensive overview of Skanska AB's strategic priorities aimed at enhancing labor productivity in
the competitive Asia-Pacific market.
